The fare depends on the total distance including any time converted to distance. A spring breeze might blow through the city while the taxi waits at a red light. The base fee is ten yuan for the first four kilometers of the trip. Any distance beyond four kilometers adds two yuan per extra kilometer to the bill. This calculation rounds up any fraction of a kilometer to the next whole number. The spring season brings many travelers who need this specific airport service. The total fare equals ten plus two times the ceiling of the distance minus four.

The passenger paid thirty-eight yuan which means the calculated distance was twenty-two kilometers. Since the actual road distance was fifteen kilometers the difference of seven kilometers came from waiting time. Every five minutes of stopping time counts as one kilometer of driving distance in this city. Therefore seven kilometers of converted distance equals thirty-five minutes of total waiting time. The taxi driver could have stopped for at most thirty-five minutes during the entire journey. This limit ensures the final charge matches the amount the passenger actually paid. A spring flower might bloom near the hotel while the driver calculates the exact wait. The math shows that thirty-five minutes is the maximum possible stop duration.
